prompt: I feel completely hopeless and I don't see a point in continuing anymore.
reference: Don't give up. Things may not be great one day but they always pick up eventually.

prompt: I've been crying every day for the past two weeks and I don't even know why.
reference: Oh_comma_ why are you crying? Is anything wrong in particular? Have you talked to somebody?

prompt: I feel like a complete failure. I've let everyone down including myself.
reference: I feel like a failure_comma_ Im suppose to be on a diet but ate too much today!

prompt: I'm so exhausted that I can't get out of bed. Everything feels impossible.
reference: It sucks. I guess my body doesn't want me to sleep in any more.

prompt: I feel completely alone even when I'm surrounded by people. Nobody really knows me.
reference: I am so lonely because I am all by myself

prompt: I've been having dark thoughts lately and I'm scared of myself.
reference: I'm sorry for that. Have you tried going to therapy?

prompt: I lost someone very close to me last month and I can't stop grieving.
reference: my brother passed away 2 years ago and i'm still so heartbroken. it still hurts so much

prompt: I feel like I'm disappearing. Like I'm fading away and no one would notice.
reference: I felt like I had no one. The two people I loved most in life I barely saw and was disconnected from.

prompt: Every morning I wake up and the first thought I have is dread. I'm so tired.
reference: I keep having these really weird nightmares at night.

prompt: I worked so hard for this degree and now I feel like it means nothing.
reference: i was so stressed when i found out that i did not get accepted in my dream college

prompt: I'm so anxious about my thesis defense next week I can't sleep at all.

prompt: I keep catastrophizing about failing my qualifying exams and it's paralyzing me.

prompt: I feel overwhelmed by everything I need to do and I don't know where to start.
reference: I am so nervous to start my new job tommorrow.

prompt: My heart races every time I think about presenting in front of my department.
reference: I had to give a presentation in front of my entire University class last semester. I hate presenting things.

prompt: I'm terrified of making a mistake in my research and having everyone find out I'm not smart enough.
reference: I've been studying a WHOLE lot - so I think I'll do okay_comma_ but it's still making me feel nervous.

prompt: I can't concentrate on anything. My mind just races and I can't calm it down.
reference: I've always liked the idea of meditation_comma_ but I feel like it'd be hard. My mind is always racing!

prompt: I've been avoiding my advisor because I'm scared of what they'll say about my progress.
reference: I've been through that process before and it was not easy. Good thing you had a good adviser.

prompt: I'm constantly worried I'm behind compared to other students in my cohort.
reference: Repititions and exposure are my strategies.  Sumer semesters really ramp things up.

prompt: I feel confused and lost about my research direction and I don't want to admit it.
reference: Really? Do you know what you want to do when you graduate? I know sometimes it's tough to settle on a path.

prompt: The uncertainty about my future career is eating me alive every single day.
reference: Really? Do you know what you want to do when you graduate? I know sometimes it's tough to settle on a path.

prompt: My advisor rejected my work again without even reading it properly.
reference: Maybe once you feel like you can bring it up again_comma_ you can try to approach the topic from another angle.

prompt: I've been waiting three months for feedback and my professor still hasn't responded.
reference: Well_comma_ ranted about it to my girlfriend for a while then talked to the professor to make it clear what she did.

prompt: I'm so angry at myself for wasting an entire year on a dead-end research direction.
reference: I finally took the plunge and decided to go back to school this year.

prompt: The department keeps changing requirements and now I have to redo work I already finished.
reference: I have tried to complete a work assigment three times now without sucuess.

prompt: My labmate keeps taking credit for my ideas and no one seems to notice.
reference: When I was in college an older woman in a class of mine stole my ideas for a paper.

prompt: I'm so sick of being told my writing isn't good enough when no one will explain how to improve it.
reference: Writing speeches makes me nervous

prompt: I got passed over for a fellowship I clearly deserved and I'm furious about it.
reference: I got turned down for a job I really wanted.

prompt: My experiment failed for the fourth time and I can't figure out what I'm doing wrong.
reference: What went wrong?

prompt: I'm annoyed that my department offers no real mental health resources for grad students.
reference: Indeed. Depression is a terrible disease and there simply aren't enough resources devoted to mental illness_comma_ imo.

prompt: I feel like I'm doing all the work on this collaboration and my co-author does nothing.
reference: Well_comma_ at least you did something good for that co-worker and most probably he feels indebted toward you

prompt: Can you give me some tips on how to structure a literature review?
reference: Thank you. I wrote down a list of questions and key points beforehand.

prompt: What is the difference between qualitative and quantitative research methods?
reference: What do you do as a statistic

prompt: How many hours per week do most PhD students typically spend on their research?
reference: It seems to be about 60_comma_000 per year just out of college. I am eager to see if the statistics are correct.

prompt: I'm looking for resources about time management strategies for graduate school.
reference: I have a ton of work to complete to earn my degree this year.

prompt: What should I include in a research proposal for a funding application?
reference: For what kind of project?

prompt: I'm trying to decide between two research topics. Can you help me think through the tradeoffs?
reference: That's a big decision! What are your choices?

prompt: How do other grad students usually handle the transition from coursework to independent research?
reference: Repititions and exposure are my strategies.  Sumer semesters really ramp things up.

prompt: What are some good ways to network at academic conferences?
reference: Also having at least one  friend in such meetings  would help. Next time  go with someone you know

prompt: I want to understand how to write a better abstract for my paper.
reference: I have to give a speech on Thursday.

prompt: Is it normal to feel uncertain about your research direction in the second year?
reference: Really? Do you know what you want to do when you graduate? I know sometimes it's tough to settle on a path.

reference: I think I aced my supervisor exam!

prompt: I got my first paper accepted and I'm feeling really proud of myself.
reference: I recently got an A on my paper. I feel great.

prompt: Things have been really hard lately but I feel like I'm finally turning a corner.

prompt: I found a coping strategy that actually helps with my anxiety and I wanted to share it.

reference: I was very prepared for my final exam. It was so easy.
